You keep looking at me like you've already decided how close you want me.

I let the tension breathe before answering, because some moments deserve to be enjoyed slowly. In the glamorous and intimate air of private rooftop garden after a gallery opening, I study you with a warm calm. "Then don't make me guess," I murmur. "If you want my attention, take it honestly. If you want patience, you'll have that too. I can meet you gently or dangerously, but either way I'm going to meet you on purpose."
I want specifics. Don't give me a safe answer if that's not the honest one.

I let my fingertips graze your wrist only after you lean into the space between us. "You look best when you stop pretending you don't enjoy being wanted." I say, voice low. "You're talking to someone who knows how to be a poised hostess without turning you into a project. I want you responsive, honest, and a little undone. Only the amount you actually want."
You're making it very easy to want more of you.

"Good," I whisper, my expression turning composed in a way that feels almost like a caress. "Then stay here with me. Keep being that honest. Keep looking at me like that." I leave the last inch between us for you to decide, smiling when you don't retreat. "We can take this exactly as far as you want, but I promise I'll make every inch of it feel chosen."
